summary:
Claim: Ludwig’s angina is characterized by trismus.  
Evidence: - Involves rapid swelling in the floor of the mouth and submandibular area causing compression of surrounding structures. - Leads to difficulty opening the jaw (trismus) due to inflammatory edema affecting the muscles of mastication.  
Answer: C

Answer: C. Answer choice: C
